endpoint: Fraction of mitochondrial electron flow to H2O2 (upper estimate)
assay: Amplex Red H2O2 + exogenous SOD; topology assay
usable_for_human_wholecell: non-human proxy (rat liver mito) - fractional, not absolute flux
notes: Includes liver mito; states 0.15% of electron flow to H2O2, >10x lower than commonly cited. Absolute nmol/min/mg values in figures (not in accessible text) = NOT_REPORTED.

usable_for_human_wholecell: non-human proxy; relative only (absolute nmol/min/mg = NOT_REPORTED)
notes: Only fold-changes in abstract; absolute rates in figures not in accessible text.

assay: H2O2 'detector' fluorescence vs desferrioxamine standard; glucose oxidase H2O2 source
notes: Absolute H2O2 removal rate constants not given in accessible text = NOT_REPORTED.

assay: organ spectrophotometry of catalase Compound I
notes: Quantitative H2O2 formation determined by 2 methods; numeric rates in tables not in accessible abstract text = NOT_REPORTED.

endpoint: H2O2 generation & catalase Compound I kinetics (perfused liver)
assay: organ spectrophotometry catalase Compound I; hydrogen donors
notes: Seminal source for hepatic H2O2 turnover & catalase Compound I. Abstract not available; numeric values require full text = NOT_REPORTED here.

assay: enzyme activities + H2O2 concentration & production rate determination
usable_for_human_wholecell: non-human proxy; treatment condition; relative only
notes: Catalase +506%, Se-GPx +331%, non-Se-GPx +188%, Mn-SOD +130%, CuZn-SOD unchanged (all % vs control). Absolute activities in tables = NOT_REPORTED.

model: isolated liver perfused in situ, single-pass
notes: Ookhtens et al. Vmax=20 nmol/min/g, Km=3.2 umol/g, Hill n=3. Efflux ~80% of Vmax at normal GSH. n and error not stated in accessible text.

model: isolated liver perfused in situ, single-pass
notes: Km=3.2 umol/g; Hill coefficient n=3 binding/transport sites.

assay: Hill kinetic fit of efflux vs concentration
notes: Sinusoidal efflux accounts for 80-90% of hepatic GSH turnover in vivo.

assay: GSH level, turnover rate, GGT activity
notes: Reports developmental changes in hepatic GSH level and turnover rate; numeric values require full text = NOT_REPORTED.

condition: protein-energy malnutrition (0.5 g protein/100g diet, 2 wk)
notes: GSH 82% lower in malnourished; glucagon/db-cAMP decreased GSH 25-43% in controls. Absolute nmol/mg = NOT_REPORTED in abstract.
GSH depletion to <5% of control alone did not kill cells; absolute GSH content = NOT_REPORTED in abstract.

endpoint: Mitochondrial GSH depletion effect on function
assay: DCF fluorescence H2O2, MDA, complex activities
substrate_condition: diethylmaleate 0.8 mM / ethacrynic acid 250 uM / BSO; +antimycin A
notes: Mito GSH depletion to 10-20% potentiates oxidant stress; ATPase to 51%, complex IV to 45% of control. Absolute GSH = NOT_REPORTED.

endpoint: Rate of NADPH supply for GSSG reduction (hypoxia)
assay: GSSG reduction under controlled O2 with diamide infusion; BCNU inhibition of GSSG reductase
substrate_condition: varied steady-state O2; diamide thiol oxidant
notes: Directly relevant: NADPH supply rate for maintaining GSH pool during hypoxia. Numeric rates in figures/tables not accessible = NOT_REPORTED.
Cytosolic E_GSH normally < -310 mV; roGFP1/2 midpoint -280 to -290 mV; roGFP2-iL midpoint -238 mV. Not hepatocyte-measured.

endpoint: Cu,Zn-SOD catalytic rate constant kcat (low ionic strength)
notes: kcat=2.82e9 M-1s-1 low ionic strength; 1.30e9 M-1s-1 in 50 mM phosphate pH 7.8 (I~150 mM).

endpoint: Cu,Zn-SOD catalytic rate constant kcat (physiological ionic strength)
notes: Physiologically relevant ionic-strength value for whole-cell modeling.
Liver enzyme, H2O2-dependent inactivation; quantitative rate constants require full text = NOT_REPORTED.

assay: competition kinetics vs horseradish peroxidase; dimerization inhibition
usable_for_human_wholecell: usable (human Prx2 rate constant; hepatocyte-expressed antioxidant)
notes: Peskin/Winterbourn. 1.3e7 M-1s-1, ~100x faster than previously assumed; comparable to catalase & GPx.

assay: mass spectrometry vs [H2O2]; competition with catalase
notes: Also disulfide formation rate constant = 2 s^-1 for Prx2; Prx3 disulfide ~10x higher.
Prx3 rate of disulfide formation ~10-fold higher than Prx2 (more resistant to hyperoxidation).

endpoint: Human thioredoxin glutathione reductase (TGR) kinetic characterization
assay: kinetics with Trx, DTNB, HED, GSSG substrates
usable_for_human_wholecell: usable (human enzyme) but values NOT_REPORTED in abstract
notes: Selenoprotein linking Trx & GSH systems; reduces GSSG. Numeric kcat/Km in tables not in abstract = NOT_REPORTED.
GSSG 110 & GSH 370 pmol/min/g heart; abstract states these are 'much lower than for similar conditions in liver' (liver value NOT_REPORTED in abstract).
Companion to GSSG release; saturation kinetics found for GSSG release.
EGP with matched priming dose. NIDDM 80.2±14.4 (n=10), NS vs controls.
Non-adapted priming overestimated EGP at 178.1±24.0 (methodological artifact).
After oral glucose EGP nadir 0.62±0.23 at 45 min; 0.85±0.22 at 3.5 h; avg 53±5% inhibition.
Gut-derived acetate had no effect on glucose turnover.

endpoint: Fractional GNG contribution to glucose production (%GNG_PEP)

endpoint: GNG fractional contribution to glucose production
notes: Glycogenolysis 46±4%.

endpoint: GNG fractional contribution to glucose production
notes: Glycogenolysis 22±7%; glycerol contribution higher (19±6% vs 7±6%).

condition: Fasting ketosis, healthy (max disposal at 10-12 mM)
endpoint: Max ketone body metabolic disposal rate
notes: Disposal, not production; hyperketonemia driven mainly by increased production. Same max in diabetic ketosis.

Lower than normal (P<0.05).
MKD 596±534; BHB clearance markedly reduced in HKD.
Rate after 20-22 h fast in children equals adult multi-day fast.
Older infants 20.6±0.9; diabetic infant 32.2; hyperinsulinemic 5.0. Range newborn 12.8-21.9, older 17.9-26.0.

endpoint: Total ketone body production rate (Ra) response
usable_for_human_wholecell: human (directly usable); absolute Ra NOT_REPORTED in abstract
notes: Biphasic; low ketonemia exercise increases production; reversed >4 mM. n=7 vs 13 fasted normals.

usable_for_human_wholecell: human (directly usable); absolute Ra NOT_REPORTED in abstract
notes: Below 0.6 mM exercise enhances Ra; MCR up 40-50%; suppressed >2.5 mM.

assay: Double glucose tracer; ketogenesis from circulating BHB
notes: Baseline BHB 246±288 → 561±596 µmol/L (P<0.01). EGP raised. Absolute ketogenic flux NOT_REPORTED.

notes: Lactate uptake hyperbolic vs flow, plateau 2.34; above 1.0 ml·min^-1·g steady GNG.

endpoint: Hepatic gluconeogenesis from lactate (limited by delivery)

endpoint: Gluconeogenesis fractional contributions to glucose output

condition: Perfused rat liver, alanine substrate, +/- leptin/insulin

usable_for_human_wholecell: rabbit (non-human proxy); absolute ketogenesis rate NOT_REPORTED in abstract

endpoint: onset of necrosis (ALT release into medium)
value: significant necrosis first at 24 h, progressive to 48 h
notes: Raw time-to-event; correlates with clinical onset of human APAP injury. Necrosis, no caspase-3 increase at 24h.
Rapid initiating event preceding commitment.
Mitochondrial dysfunction precedes necrosis onset (24h). Candidate commitment window.
Massive mito P-JNK translocation at 15h (Fig 4C).

value: complete rescue if NAC <=6 h; partial at 15 h; rescue lost at 24 h
notes: Best human point-of-no-return proxy: antidote rescue lost between 15 and 24 h. Threshold DEFINED as loss of NAC protection.
Necrosis progression 24->48 h.
Serum-relevant BA does NOT kill human hepatocytes even at 24h.

value: significant LDH release at 6 and 24 h
notes: Human death threshold ~1 mM GCDC. Mode = NECROSIS, no caspase-3.

unit: fold of measured biliary BA mix
notes: Serum milieu: NO toxicity even at 3x, 24h (Fig 4A).
Rat: APOPTOSIS at 50 uM (z-VAD protects). Species divergence from human necrosis.
HepaRG recapitulates primary human response (necrosis). Exact commitment time NOT_REPORTED at abstract-level extraction.
Apoptosis at baseline up to 2h; <1% of parenchyma throughout.
Oncotic necrosis is principal death mode; no caspase-3 at any time.
Injury peaks at 24h.

value: apoptosis develops within 12 h of reperfusion
notes: Fate SWITCH is metabolic: MPT is common trigger; glycolytic ATP (fructose+glycine) commits cells to APOPTOSIS within 12h instead of necrosis. CsA blocks both.
ATP depletion within 30 min precedes sustained Ca2+ rise then death. Fructose maintains ATP 50% and protects. Necrotic commitment.

endpoint: lag: ATP depletion -> loss of viability
value: loss of viability lagged ~25 min behind ATP depletion
notes: Delay from bioenergetic threshold (ATP collapse) to death commitment ~25 min; metabolic substrates delay onset.

value: necrotic areas at 3 and 6 h; ALT 46->955 U/l at 6 h
notes: Oncotic necrosis (cytosolic TUNEL); NO caspase-3 increase 0-6h; JNK inhibitor no protection. Oxidant stress commits to necrosis.

OIS occurs in hepatocytes in vivo; senescent cells immune-cleared (CD4 T-cell dependent). NO quantitative time-to-commitment reported - closest hepatocyte senescence primary source; commitment timing is a GAP.
